region_of_origin: India (South and North Indian cooking) / Mediterranean
food_subgroup: Coarsely milled durum wheat endosperm (semolina), dry raw

ontology.foodex2_description: Semolina (durum wheat, coarsely milled endosperm, raw)
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for raw semolina in available clinical terminology resources; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient for this record.

serving.description: 50g dry (standard dry measure for one serving of upma or porridge)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of dry semolina is approximately 50g, which yields roughly one cup of cooked upma or porridge when water and aromatics are added.

nutrient_source.title: Semolina / Rava — HPB Singapore Foodo Database, Food Code F05080017; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 169747, Semolina, unenriched) and ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; USDA Agricultural Research Service; Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo Food Code F05080017 is an exact match for raw uncooked semolina from durum wheat. Values cross-checked against USDA FDC ID 169747 (Semolina, unenriched) and ICMR-NIN; all three sources are in close agreement. HPB values used as primary; USDA used for micronutrient cross-validation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — University of Sydney GI Database; cooked semolina porridge and upma (GI approximately 65)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 65 is referenced from the University of Sydney GI Database for cooked semolina porridge and upma preparations. Dry raw semolina has no direct GI measurement (GI is only meaningful for consumed/cooked food); the GI of 65 applies to the cooked product. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (65 × 72.8 × 0.50) / 100 = 23.7, rounded to 23.6 per input record. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (1 mg/100g) with beneficial potassium (186 mg/100g) and magnesium (47 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Semolina is virtually sodium-free at 1 mg per 100g and provides potassium and magnesium that support vascular function, making it a suitable staple grain for individuals managing hypertension, provided it is prepared without added salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~65) moderated by durum wheat protein matrix; higher than wholegrain alternatives
type2_diabetes.guidance: Cooked semolina dishes such as upma have a moderate GI of approximately 65, which is lower than white rice or white bread due to the dense protein matrix of durum wheat; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume semolina in controlled portions (50g dry) and pair it with vegetables or legumes to further blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.2g/100g), zero trans fat, and moderate dietary fibre (3.9g/100g) with selenium (47.3 mcg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Semolina is low in saturated fat and contains no trans fat, with 3.9g dietary fibre per 100g and a notable selenium content of 47.3 mcg per 100g, supporting antioxidant defence; it is a heart-neutral grain staple suitable for cardiovascular disease management when prepared without added fats or salt.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Selenium (47.3 mcg/100g) provides antioxidant support; contains gluten which may be relevant for some patients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Semolina provides selenium at 47.3 mcg per 100g, which supports antioxidant pathways relevant to inflammatory conditions; however, it contains gluten and individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who also have gluten sensitivity should avoid it.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Semolina (also known as rava or sooji) is the coarsely milled endosperm of durum wheat, appearing as pale cream-yellow granules with a slightly gritty, sandy texture and a mild, nutty wheat flavour. It is produced by grinding durum wheat and sifting out the bran and germ, leaving the starchy endosperm in coarse particles. In Indian cooking, semolina is the primary ingredient for upma (a savoury semolina porridge cooked with mustard seeds, curry leaves, onions, and vegetables), rava dosa (thin, crispy crepes made from a fermented semolina batter), rava idli (steamed semolina cakes), and kesari or halwa (sweet semolina pudding cooked with ghee, sugar, and cardamom). In Mediterranean and Middle Eastern cuisines, semolina is the base for pasta, couscous, and Arabic sweets such as basbousa. Dry-roasting rava in a pan before adding water is standard practice in South Indian cooking — it deepens the nutty flavour and prevents clumping during cooking. Nutritionally, raw semolina provides 360 kcal, 12.7g protein, 72.8g carbohydrate, 3.9g dietary fibre, and only 1.1g fat per 100g. It is notably rich in selenium (47.3 mcg/100g), folate (72 mcg/100g), and phosphorus (136 mg/100g). Its moderate glycemic index of approximately 65 (when cooked) makes it a better white starch choice than plain white rice or white bread, though it is inferior to whole grain alternatives for glycaemic management. Semolina contains gluten and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or gluten intolerance. It is naturally vegan, halal, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: sooji vs rice which is healthier

confidence_reason: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo Database (Food Code F05080017) for raw uncooked semolina from durum wheat. Values cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 169747 (Semolina, unenriched) and ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods. All three approved sources are in close agreement. GI referenced from University of Sydney GI Database for cooked semolina preparations.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for semolina not separately reported in HPB Foodo F05080017; USDA FDC 169747 reports monounsaturated fat at approximately 0.15g and polyunsaturated fat at approximately 0.43g per 100g but these values were not included in the primary HPB source and are therefore set to -1 pending source reconciliation. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo F05080017 primary record; USDA reports approximately 12.7g water per 100g for unenriched semolina. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo and USDA FDC for unenriched semolina). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — semolina is a plant-derived grain product containing no cholesterol). vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are 0.0 (confirmed zero or below detection threshold per HPB Foodo F05080017 and USDA FDC 169747 for unenriched semolina). GI of 65 applies to cooked semolina preparations; dry raw semolina has no direct GI measurement. Glycemic load per serving calculated at 50g dry portion: (65 × 72.8 × 0.50) / 100 ≈ 23.6. Semolina contains gluten and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or wheat allergy.
